```markdown
## Security fixes

The following updates were made to address security issues:

### 2.8.2

- Updated Go to version 1.24.4. [[PR 5323](https://github.com/grafana/tempo/pull/5323)]

### 2.8.0

- Used distroless base container images for improved security. ([PR 4556](https://github.com/grafana/tempo/pull/4556))
- Updated to go 1.24.3. ([PR 5110](https://github.com/grafana/tempo/pull/5110))
```

The format is:
- Bullet list items
- Each item has a description of the fix
- Optionally links to CVEs/security advisories
- PR number at the end in brackets or parentheses with link to GitHub PR

```212:217:docs/sources/tempo/release-notes/v2-9.md
### 2.9.1

- Updated Go to version 1.25.5 to address CVE. [[PR 6233](https://github.com/grafana/tempo/pull/6233)]
- Updated `golang.org/x/crypto` to address CVE. [[PR 6235](https://github.com/grafana/tempo/pull/6235)]
- Updated `github.com/expr-lang/expr` to v1.17.7 to address security vulnerability. [[PR 6234](https://github.com/grafana/tempo/pull/6234)]
```

{"id":3109100010,"number":6092,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"chore(deps): update module github.com/expr-lang/expr to v1.17.7 [security]","body":"This PR contains the following updates:\n\n| Package | Change | [Age](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Confidence](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| [github.com/expr-lang/expr](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr) | `v1.17.6` -\u0026gt; `v1.17.7` | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/go/github.com%2fexpr-lang%2fexpr/v1.17.7?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/go/github.com%2fexpr-lang%2fexpr/v1.17.6/v1.17.7?slim=true) |\n\n---\n\n\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\n\u0026gt; Some dependencies could not be looked up. Check the Dependency Dashboard for more information.\n\n### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts\n\n#### [CVE-2025-68156](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/security/advisories/GHSA-cfpf-hrx2-8rv6)\n\nSeveral builtin functions in Expr, including `flatten`, `min`, `max`, `mean`, and `median`, perform\nrecursive traversal over user-provided data structures without enforcing a maximum recursion depth.\n\nIf the evaluation environment contains **deeply nested** or **cyclic** data structures, these functions may recurse\nindefinitely until exceed the Go runtime stack limit. This results in a **stack overflow panic**, causing the host\napplication to crash.\n\nWhile exploitability depends on whether an attacker can influence or inject cyclic or pathologically deep data into the\nevaluation environment, this behavior represents a denial-of-service (DoS) risk and affects overall library robustness.\nInstead of returning a recoverable evaluation error, the process may terminate unexpectedly.\n\n### Impact\n\nIn affected versions, evaluation of expressions that invoke certain builtin functions on untrusted or insufficiently\nvalidated data structures can lead to a **process-level crash** due to stack exhaustion.\n\nThis issue is most relevant in scenarios where:\n\n* Expr is used to evaluate expressions against externally supplied or dynamically constructed environments.\n* Cyclic references (directly or indirectly) can be introduced into arrays, maps, or structs.\n* There are no application-level safeguards preventing deeply nested input data.\n\nIn typical use cases with controlled, acyclic data, the issue may not manifest. However, when present, the resulting\npanic can be used to reliably crash the application, constituting a denial of service.\n\n### Patches\n\nThe issue has been fixed in the v1.17.7 versions of Expr.\n\nThe patch introduces a **maximum recursion depth limit** for affected builtin functions. When this limit is exceeded,\nevaluation aborts gracefully and returns a descriptive error instead of panicking.\n\nAdditionally, the maximum depth can be customized by users via `builtin.MaxDepth`, allowing applications with legitimate\ndeep structures to raise the limit in a controlled manner.\n\nUsers are strongly encouraged to upgrade to the patched release, which includes both the recursion guard and\ncomprehensive test coverage to prevent regressions.\n\n### Workarounds\n\nFor users who cannot immediately upgrade, the following mitigations are recommended:\n\n* Ensure that evaluation environments cannot contain cyclic references.\n* Validate or sanitize externally supplied data structures before passing them to Expr.\n* Wrap expression evaluation with panic recovery to prevent a full process crash (as a last-resort defensive measure).\n\nThese workarounds reduce risk but do not fully eliminate the issue without the patch.\n\n---\n\n### Expr has Denial of Service via Unbounded Recursion in Builtin Functions\n[CVE-2025-68156](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68156) / [GHSA-cfpf-hrx2-8rv6](https://redirect.github.com/advisories/GHSA-cfpf-hrx2-8rv6)\n\n\nMore information\n\n#### Details\nSeveral builtin functions in Expr, including `flatten`, `min`, `max`, `mean`, and `median`, perform\nrecursive traversal over user-provided data structures without enforcing a maximum recursion depth.\n\nIf the evaluation environment contains **deeply nested** or **cyclic** data structures, these functions may recurse\nindefinitely until exceed the Go runtime stack limit. This results in a **stack overflow panic**, causing the host\napplication to crash.\n\nWhile exploitability depends on whether an attacker can influence or inject cyclic or pathologically deep data into the\nevaluation environment, this behavior represents a denial-of-service (DoS) risk and affects overall library robustness.\nInstead of returning a recoverable evaluation error, the process may terminate unexpectedly.\n\n##### Impact\n\nIn affected versions, evaluation of expressions that invoke certain builtin functions on untrusted or insufficiently\nvalidated data structures can lead to a **process-level crash** due to stack exhaustion.\n\nThis issue is most relevant in scenarios where:\n\n* Expr is used to evaluate expressions against externally supplied or dynamically constructed environments.\n* Cyclic references (directly or indirectly) can be introduced into arrays, maps, or structs.\n* There are no application-level safeguards preventing deeply nested input data.\n\nIn typical use cases with controlled, acyclic data, the issue may not manifest. However, when present, the resulting\npanic can be used to reliably crash the application, constituting a denial of service.\n\n##### Patches\n\nThe issue has been fixed in the v1.17.7 versions of Expr.\n\nThe patch introduces a **maximum recursion depth limit** for affected builtin functions. When this limit is exceeded,\nevaluation aborts gracefully and returns a descriptive error instead of panicking.\n\nAdditionally, the maximum depth can be customized by users via `builtin.MaxDepth`, allowing applications with legitimate\ndeep structures to raise the limit in a controlled manner.\n\nUsers are strongly encouraged to upgrade to the patched release, which includes both the recursion guard and\ncomprehensive test coverage to prevent regressions.\n\n##### Workarounds\n\nFor users who cannot immediately upgrade, the following mitigations are recommended:\n\n* Ensure that evaluation environments cannot contain cyclic references.\n* Validate or sanitize externally supplied data structures before passing them to Expr.\n* Wrap expression evaluation with panic recovery to prevent a full process crash (as a last-resort defensive measure).\n\nThese workarounds reduce risk but do not fully eliminate the issue without the patch.\n\n#### Severity\n- CVSS Score: 7.5 / 10 (High)\n- Vector String: `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H`\n\n#### References\n- [https://github.com/expr-lang/expr/security/advisories/GHSA-cfpf-hrx2-8rv6](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/security/advisories/GHSA-cfpf-hrx2-8rv6)\n- [https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68156](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68156)\n- [https://github.com/expr-lang/expr/pull/870](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/pull/870)\n- [https://github.com/expr-lang/expr](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr)\n\nThis data is provided by [OSV](https://osv.dev/vulnerability/GHSA-cfpf-hrx2-8rv6) and the [GitHub Advisory Database](https://redirect.github.com/github/advisory-database) ([CC-BY 4.0](https://redirect.github.com/github/advisory-database/blob/main/LICENSE.md)).\n\n\n---\n\n### Release Notes\n\n\nexpr-lang/expr (github.com/expr-lang/expr)\n\n### [`v1.17.7`](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/releases/tag/v1.17.7)\n\n[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/compare/v1.17.6...v1.17.7)\n\n**Expr** is a Go-centric expression language designed to deliver dynamic configurations with unparalleled accuracy,\nsafety, and speed.\n\n```go\nprogram, err := expr.Compile(`let foo = bar(); baz(foo, foo)`)\n```\n\nThis release brings new language features, **performance improvements** across runtime and compiler, better error\nhandling, and many important bug fixes.\n\n#### New Features\n\n##### **Support for `else if` expressions**\n\n- You can now chain conditional branches using `else if`! ([#​879](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/879))\n\n##### **Unicode escapes in the `\\u{XXXXXX}` format**\n\n- String literals now support Unicode code point escapes such as `\\u{1F600}`. ([#​882](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/882))\n\n##### **Byte slice support in the `matches` operator**\n\nThe `matches` operator now works with `[]byte`, improving interoperability with binary data. ([#​876](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/876))\n\n##### **Short-circuit control options**\n\nNew options allow enabling or disabling short-circuiting behavior in the compiler and VM. ([#​847](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/847))\n\n##### **Option to disable `if` operator**\n\nA separate `DisableIfOperator` option is now available. ([#​881](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/881))\n\n#### Performance Improvements\n\n##### **Runtime structure fetch improvements**\n\nAccessing struct fields at runtime is now faster. ([#​833](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/833))\n\n##### **VM function call optimizations**\n\nFunction calls inside the VM execute more efficiently. ([#​832](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/832))\n\n##### **Type system performance boost**\n\nLarge or complex type operations now run significantly faster. ([#​824](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/824))\n\n#### Bug Fixes\n\n- Guard negative forward jump offsets (*[#​861](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/861)*).\n- Prevent stack underflow in the VM (*[#​860](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/860)*).\n- Correct behavior of `AsBool` with nil and undefined variables (*[#​866](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/866)*).\n- Proper handling of nil arguments in variadic functions (*[#​868](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/868)*).\n- Show function name for safe calls in disassembly (*[#​869](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/869)*).\n- Limit recursion depth in built-in functions (*[#​870](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/870)*).\n- Throw an error when a predicate is missing in the parser (*[#​842](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/842)*).\n- Fixed array type resolution in conditional operator (*[#​865](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/865)*).\n- Disallow access to unexported struct fields (*[#​846](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/846)*).\n- Fixed retrieving field pointers in structs (*[#​843](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/843)*).\n- Better error position reporting in multi-line scripts (*[#​827](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/827)*).\n- Support quoting backticks inside string literals (*[#​820](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/820)*).\n- Fixed auto-dereference for maps/slices and conditionals (*[#​871](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/871)*).\n- Wrap merged optimizer results as predicates (*[#​864](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/864)*).\n- Improved missing property handling (*[#​854](https://redirect.github.com/expr-lang/expr/issues/854)*).\n- Handle invalid `if` conditions and detect null-byte crashes.\n- Added regression tests for several reported issues.\n\n\n\n---\n\n### Configuration\n\n📅 **Schedule**: Branch creation - \u0026#34;\u0026#34; (UTC), Automerge - At any time (no schedule defined).\n\n🚦 **Automerge**: Enabled.\n\n♻ **Rebasing**: Whenever PR is behind base branch, or you tick the rebase/retry checkbox.\n\n🔕 **Ignore**: Close this PR and you won\u0026#39;t be reminded about this update again.\n\n---\n\n - [ ] If you want to rebase/retry this PR, check this box\n\n---\n\n## Need help?\nYou can ask for more help in the following Slack channel: #proj-renovate-self-hosted. In that channel you can also find ADR and FAQ docs in the Resources
